title: We Can Want Different Things and Find Common Ground
subtitle: An energy statement for releasing the belief that different money histories make a partnership financially incompatible
excerpt: A short energy statement for the recurring money tension in a partnership. The release names the belief that incompatible money histories mean one person must lose; the allow names a shared financial life built on found rather than imposed common ground.
sourceNotes: Original energy statement written for homemade.education. The release-and-allow framework is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Use when money disagreements with a partner feel structural rather than resolvable — when different histories feel like incompatibility.

content.text: An energy statement for the money tension that comes from two different financial histories sharing one life. The release names the belief that different histories are irreconcilable; the allow names the alternative: a shared financial life where common ground is found rather than imposed.

I am ready to release the belief that our different relationships with money mean we are fundamentally incompatible, that one of us must be wrong, or that this tension cannot be resolved without one of us giving up something essential. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.

I am ready to align with and allow a money relationship in which our different histories can coexist, in which shared ground is found rather than imposed, and in which our different views are a starting point rather than a verdict. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Say each three times, slow, out loud or in your mind.
Written for homemade.education. The release-and-allow structure is from The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
